Scenario.Description: Launch Brave with CDP enabled on port 9222. Required before EXEC/FILL/CLICKID. Do not run if a CDP-enabled browser is already open.

Command (PowerShell): Start-Process brave -ArgumentList "--remote-debugging-port=9222","--user-data-dir=$env:TEMP\aiapi-9222","{{pgadmin_url}}login" Only the instance started with --remote-debugging-port=9222 accepts CDP commands. If two Brave instances exist (one CDP, one normal), proc:'brave.exe' may hit either. Kill all stale Brave processes before launching: Stop-Process -Name brave -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue Wait ~3 s after launch before sending EXEC.

Navigate to the pgAdmin login page, fill email and password, and submit. After success the browser is redirected to /browser/ (the object-explorer page). WARNING: Do NOT use NAVIGATE while already on /browser/ — this triggers the 'Opustit web?' (Leave page?) dialog which cannot be programmatically dismissed.

1. NAVIGATE to {{pgadmin_url}}login — loads the React login form. 2. EXEC: use HTMLInputElement.prototype.value setter + input event to fill email (plain FILL does NOT trigger React state updates). 3. EXEC: same technique for password field. 4. CLICKNAME "Login" — clicks the MUI Login button. 5. Wait ~3 s, then EXEC location.href to verify redirect to /browser/. Failure: location.href still contains "login" → wrong credentials or network. After redirect: LISTWINDOWS to confirm no unexpected dialogs before continuing.

Step.note: loads pgAdmin login page; React SPA
clicks the MUI Login button (first button with text 'Login')
assert: result must NOT contain 'login'; must contain '/browser/'

Scenario.Description: GET /browser/server_group/obj/ — returns JSON array of server groups. Default: [{id:2, name:"Servers"}]. Store the relevant group id as {{sgid}}.
Response is a JSON array, NOT wrapped in a .data field. The default group "Servers" typically has id=2. Requires X-pgA-CSRFToken header (read from pgAdmin.csrf_token).

Step.note: returns [{id, name}]; store Servers group id as {{sgid}} (usually 2)

Scenario.Description: Register the PostgreSQL server in pgAdmin via POST. IMPORTANT: Each POST call creates a NEW server entry (sid increments). There is no reconnect-only endpoint. This scenario always creates a fresh connection entry. For test environments this is acceptable; for production use, consider deleting stale entries first.

POST /browser/server/obj/{{sgid}}/ with JSON body: {name, host, port, db, username, password, role:'', comment:'', connect_now:true, gid:{{sgid}}} Response on success: {node:{_id:sid, connected:true, label:'name', ...}} CRITICAL: Each POST creates a new duplicate server entry. The returned .node._id is the new sid. Store it as {{sid}} for this session. POST /browser/server/connect/{sid} does NOT exist (returns 404). Server DEFINITION survives browser restart; CONNECTION does not. To reconnect an existing server without creating a duplicate: DELETE /browser/server/obj/{{sgid}}/{{old_sid}}/ first, then POST. Symptom of not being connected: database/nodes returns HTTP 500 with errormsg: 'invalid literal for int() with base 10: None' connect_now:true immediately opens a connection (verifies credentials). CSRF token required in X-pgA-CSRFToken header.
After the POST, Brave may show 'Uložit heslo?' (Save password?) dialog. Dismiss via SENDKEYS HANDLE:{{pwdlg_handle}} '{ESC}' before proceeding.
List databases on server {{sid}} in server group {{sgid}}. Find "aiapi_test" and store its _id as {{dbid}}.

GET /browser/database/nodes/{{sgid}}/{{sid}}/ Response: {data:[{_id, label, connected, _type: 'database', ...}]} OR the array directly. Use (d.data||d) to handle both shapes. aiapi_test should appear in the list; its _id field is the pgAdmin DB id. NOT /children/ — that path returns 404.

Step.note: find aiapi_test entry; store its id as {{dbid}}

Scenario.Description: List schemas in database {{dbid}} on server {{sid}}. Find "public" schema and store its _id as {{schid}} (typically OID 2200).

GET /browser/schema/nodes/{{sgid}}/{{sid}}/{{dbid}}/ Response: array of {_id (OID), label, ...}. "public" schema has OID 2200 on a standard PostgreSQL installation. Store {{schid}} for use in the table listing step.

Step.note: find public schema; store its _id as {{schid}} (usually 2200)

Scenario.Description: GET /browser/table/nodes/{{sgid}}/{{sid}}/{{dbid}}/{{schid}}/ — list tables. Assert all five required AIAPI tables exist: aiapi_users, aiapi_roles, aiapi_user_roles, aiapi_apikeys, aiapi_settings. Returns {tables:[...], missing:[], ok:true} on success.

Response: array of {_id, label, ...} where label is the table name. Build the union of all labels, check for the 5 expected names. Return {tables:allTableNames, missing:missingNames, ok:missing.length===0}. ok:true means schema is correct; ok:false means migration was not applied. Note: aiapi_migrations is a 6th table that may also be present — that is OK.
Scenario.Steps: <Step action="EXEC" target="{{browser}}" parameter="(function(){var c=pgAdmin.csrf_token;var x=new XMLHttpRequest();x.open('GET','/browser/table/nodes/{{sgid}}/{{sid}}/{{dbid}}/{{schid}}/',false);x.setRequestHeader('X-pgA-CSRFToken',c);x.setRequestHeader('Accept','application/json');x.send();var d=JSON.parse(x.responseText);var tbls=(d.data||d).map(function(t){return t.label});var want=['aiapi_users','aiapi_roles','aiapi_user_roles','aiapi_apikeys','aiapi_settings'];var missing=want.filter(function(n){return tbls.indexOf(n)<0});return JSON.stringify({tables:tbls,missing:missing,ok:missing.length===0})})()" note="assert ok:true; any missing table is a test failure"/>

Scenario.Description: Dismiss Brave's 'Uložit heslo?' (Save password?) popup that appears when pgAdmin tree nodes expand (server connection passes password through HTTP).

This dialog appears as a separate Win32 window with title 'Uložit heslo?' in LISTWINDOWS results. It is NOT a web dialog — it is Brave's native credential manager UI. Cannot be dismissed via CLICKNAME (fails). Use SENDKEYS with the dialog's HWND handle and '{ESC}'. May appear TWICE per session: once when Servers group expands, once when the server node itself expands and connects. Handle both occurrences. If the dialog handle is unknown, LISTWINDOWS first and filter by title.

Step.note: find window with title 'Uložit heslo?' to get its handle
dismiss the Save password? dialog; safe to run even if not present

Scenario.label: pgAdmin: open Query Tool for a database
Scenario.Description: Opens the pgAdmin Query Tool for the database identified by {{dbid}} by calling the REST endpoint directly, then confirms the Query Tool iframe has loaded.

Variables required: {{pgadmin_url}}, {{browser}}, {{sgid}}, {{sid}}, {{dbid}}. Variables produced: {{qt_panel_id}} — the Query Tool iframe/panel identifier (extracted from the new iframe's src URL which contains "?id=<panel_id>").
REST endpoint to open Query Tool: POST {{pgadmin_url}}browser/query_tool/initialize/{{dbid}}/{{schid}}/{{sgid}}/{{sid}}/ (no body required; sets a session-side panel; pgAdmin then renders the iframe)
Alternatively navigate pgAdmin to open via keyboard: - Focus the database node in the tree, then press Alt+Shift+Q (pgAdmin keyboard shortcut) - The Query Tool opens as a new tab panel inside the pgAdmin browser frame.
The iframe hosting the Query Tool has a src URL that includes "?id=<panel_id>" where panel_id is a hex string like "4af8...". The src path starts with "/browser/". Use READ selector="iframe[src*='query_tool']" to get the panel element; extract id from its src attribute using a JS snippet.
IMPORTANT: the Query Tool runs inside an iframe — all subsequent EXEC calls targeting the editor or result grid MUST use the iframe's contentDocument context: document.querySelector('iframe[src*="query_tool"]').contentDocument.querySelector(...)
STABLE selectors (confirmed via live session pgAdmin 4 / pgAdmin v8+): Query Tool iframe selector: iframe[src*="query_tool"] SQL editor (CodeMirror 6): .cm-editor .cm-content (contenteditable=true) Execute button: [aria-label="Execute query"] or button[data-label="Execute/Refresh"] Result grid rows: .rdg-row Result grid cells: .rdg-cell

Step.note: check if Query Tool iframe already open; returns src URL or NOT_FOUND

Scenario.label: pgAdmin: type SQL and execute in Query Tool
Scenario.Description: Types a SQL statement into the pgAdmin Query Tool editor and clicks Execute. Waits for the result grid to appear.

Variables required: {{browser}}, {{sql}}. Variables produced: none (results read by read-result-grid).
IMPORTANT: The Query Tool lives inside an iframe. All DOM access must go via the iframe's contentDocument. The JS snippet below handles this transparently.
CodeMirror 6 does NOT support document.execCommand("insertText") in all builds. Reliable approach (confirmed working): 1. Focus the .cm-content element. 2. Use the CM6 view's dispatch() to insert text: const view = document.querySelector('iframe[src*="query_tool"]') .contentDocument.querySelector('.cm-editor').CodeMirror; view.dispatch({changes:{from:0,to:view.state.doc.length,insert:'{{sql}}'}}); 3. Click [aria-label="Execute query"] button.
Confirmation dialog "Changes will be lost": if {{confirm_discard}} == "yes" click the "Discard" button (selector: button.btn-warning or text "Discard").
The result grid appears under .rdg (react-data-grid) when the query completes. Wait condition: poll for .rdg-row elements > 0 or a status message element.

Step.note: type SQL into CM6 editor inside Query Tool iframe
allow query to execute; for long queries increase this or poll .rdg-row count

Scenario.label: pgAdmin: read rows from Query Tool result grid
Scenario.Description: Reads rows and cells from the pgAdmin Query Tool result grid (react-data-grid). Returns up to {{max_rows}} rows as a JSON array of objects keyed by column header.

Variables required: {{browser}}, {{max_rows}} (default 50). Variables produced: {{result_rows}} — JSON array of row objects.
react-data-grid (rdg) uses virtualised rendering — only visible rows are in the DOM. To get all rows scroll the grid or use pgAdmin's "Download as CSV" option for large sets. For moderate result sets (<= 500 rows) the DOM approach below is reliable.
Column headers: .rdg-header-row .rdg-cell (innerText gives column name) Data rows: .rdg-row[aria-rowindex] (skip aria-rowindex="1" = header) Cells in row: row.querySelectorAll('.rdg-cell')
Status bar (row count): ifd.querySelector('.sql-editor-status-bar')?.innerText — shows "X rows" after query.
STABLE selectors (pgAdmin 4 v8+, react-data-grid 7): .rdg — grid root .rdg-header-row — single header row .rdg-row — data rows (virtualised!) .rdg-cell — cell within row

Step.note: read visible result grid rows as JSON; max_rows limits DOM scan (virtualisation caveat)

Scenario.label: pgAdmin: full DB table verification
Scenario.Description: Complete e2e: login to pgAdmin → register PostgreSQL server → confirm aiapi_test database exists → confirm all 5 aiapi_* tables are present in the public schema.

Variables required: {{pgadmin_url}}, {{email}}, {{password}}, {{browser}}, {{pg_host}}, {{pg_port}}, {{pg_user}}, {{pg_password}}. Variables produced during execution: {{sgid}}, {{sid}}, {{dbid}}, {{schid}}.
STABLE IDs for this installation (PostgreSQL OIDs — do not change unless DB dropped): pgadmin_url = http://192.168.254.16:30056/ email = ddladmin@localhost.locale browser = brave.exe pg_host = 192.168.254.16 pg_port = 5432 pg_user = ddladmin sgid = 2 ("Servers" server group — stable) dbid = 127376 (aiapi_test — stable PostgreSQL OID) schid = 2200 (public schema OID — stable)
DYNAMIC per-session: sid changes each time register-server is called (3, 4, 5...) Always read sid from register-server response .node._id
Verified result: {tables:["aiapi_apikeys","aiapi_migrations","aiapi_roles", "aiapi_settings","aiapi_user_roles","aiapi_users"],missing:[],ok:true}
Expected LISTWINDOWS events during this scenario: After Servers expand: HANDLE:?? 'Uložit heslo?' — ESC to dismiss After server-node expand: HANDLE:?? 'Uložit heslo?' — ESC to dismiss again Both dismissals needed before tree is fully visible in pgAdmin UI.
